video:video.video:title: Stocks open sharply higher on tentative deal for end to Iran war
video:video.video:description: Stock markets are rallying worldwide and oil prices eased after the United States and Iran reached a tentative deal to extend their ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz to get the global flow of crude going again.

video:video.video:title: What you need to know about Strait of Hormuz as Iran and US reach tentative deal
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran reached an initial agreement early Monday to open the Strait of Hormuz and further extend a shaky ceasefire in the Iran war, potentially allowing desperately needed oil and natural gas to reach the global market.

video:video.video:title: Japan's prodigy DJ Rinoka still going strong at age 9
video:video.video:description: DJ Rinoka is a celebrated techno artist — at just nine-years-old. She decided to become a DJ at four after watching YouTube videos.

video:video.video:title: G7 leaders likely to discuss Iran at summit in France
video:video.video:description: World leaders began gathering in a French spa town Monday for a summit of the Group of Seven club of powerful democracies with a new impetus following President Donald Trump's announcement of an agreement that he says will bring an end to the U.S. war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Fox Corporation to buy Roku in a $22 billion deal, boosting streaming reach
video:video.video:description: Fox Corp. has agreed to buy the streaming pioneer Roku in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $22 billion, including debt. (AP Production: Marissa Duhaney)

video:video.video:title: UK bans under-16s from using social media apps including TikTok and YouTube
video:video.video:description: Britain will ban children aged under 16 from using a range of social media apps, including Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube, to protect them from harmful content and excessive screen time, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Monday.

video:video.video:title: Get ready with me for tick season
video:video.video:description: Ticks can be active in any season and it’s important to check for and remove the bloodsuckers as quickly as possible — especially after you’ve been outside hiking, gardening or enjoying nature.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese head to south Lebanon as US and Iran reach tentative deal
video:video.video:description: Thousands of displaced people and their belongings were seen on Monday heading south in Lebanon, shortly after a tentative deal was announced between the US and Iran.

video:video.video:title: Iran and US reach an initial deal to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz but challenges remain
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran on Monday reached an initial agreement that would extend their shaky ceasefire and lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Trump meeting with France's Macron, says ‘great things are going to happen’ discussing Iran deal
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump started his visit to the Group of Seven summit on Monday by hailing his agreement aimed at ending the U.S. war with Iran as a potential breakthrough for global security.

video:video.video:title: World Cup fans with tickets bought online claim they were scammed
video:video.video:description: World Cup fans with online tickets who were denied entry to the Atlanta Stadium claim they were scammed of hundreds of dollars.

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives at G7 summit after announcing US-Iran deal
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump arrived at the G7 summit in the French Alps Monday after announcing a deal he says will end the U.S.-Iran war.

video:video.video:title: 9-year-old DJ takes Japan by storm
video:video.video:description: Nine-year-old DJ Rinoka is a celebrated techno artist in Japan. She decided to become a DJ at four after watching YouTube videos.

video:video.video:title: Security is tight in Evian-les-Bains ahead of G7 summit
video:video.video:description: With world leaders flying in, the G7 summit venue in Evian-les-Bains is being transformed from a placid lakeside French town to something of a security fortress, with police patrols on many streets and corners, checking passes and watching crowds.

video:video.video:title: Russian attack sets fire to centuries-old religious site in Kyiv and kills rescuers in Kharkiv
video:video.video:description: A large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine killed rescue personnel in Kharkiv and four people in the capital Kyiv on Monday as strikes set apartment buildings ablaze and sparked a fire at the Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra, one of the country’s most significant religious landmarks.

video:video.video:title: World Cup crowds go quackers for Merlín the duck
video:video.video:description: As Mexico celebrated its World Cup-opening victory over South Africa, Merlin, a two-year-old duck dressed in the national team’s colors, became an unlikely internet sensation and the tournament’s first viral, unofficial mascot.

video:video.video:title: Stocks leap worldwide, oil prices drop after the US and Iran reach a tentative deal to end war
video:video.video:description: Stock rallied worldwide, and oil prices eased after the United States and Iran reached a tentative deal to get the global flow of crude going again.

video:video.video:title: Cape Verde fans celebrate the World Cup draw while Spain supporters left disappointed
video:video.video:description: Cape Verde supporters celebrated the World Cup tie with Spain in Atlanta. But for Spain soccer fans, the draw is disappointing. (AP Video by Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:title: The fight against data centers
video:video.video:description: Tech companies and developers looking to plunge billions of dollars into ever-bigger data centers to power artificial intelligence and cloud computing are running into stiff community opposition as some people in communities say they don’t want to live next to them, or even near them.

video:video.video:title: Macron welcomes world leaders for the G7 summit in France
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed world leaders to the G7 summit in France Monday as leaders convened amid renewed focus on the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: B-52 bomber crashes after takeoff at US military base in Southern California
video:video.video:description: Officials say a B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff at a U.S. Air Force base in Southern California's Mojave Desert.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 15
video:video.video:description: Here are the top stories for Monday, June 15: Trump joins world leaders for G7 Summit in France; Oil shipments will slowly rise after U.S.

video:video.video:title: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says Trump's Justice Department is investigating him and his wife
video:video.video:description: Democratic California Gov. Gavin Newsom accused the Justice Department of launching a politically-motivated investigation into him and his wife, saying the Trump administration was targeting him as he weighs a presidential bid.

video:video.video:title: Viral Bosnian song becomes a World Cup banger
video:video.video:description: Bosnian band Dubioza Kolektiv has transformed a song about disillusionment with the American dream into a viral anthem powering Bosnia and Herzegovina's World Cup dreams.

video:video.video:title: Ivory Coast celebrates FIFA World Cup win over Ecuador
video:video.video:description: Ivory Coast made a victorious start to their World Cup campaign, with Manchester United's Amad Diallo scoring the only goal in a 1-0 win over Ecuador.

video:video.video:title: UFC brings its trademark mayhem to the White House as President Trump celebrates 80th birthday
video:video.video:description: A massive UFC event took over the White House’s South Lawn over the weekend. The event celebrated President Donald Trump’s 80th birthday and the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong starts to promote its first five-year plan in line with mainland China
video:video.video:description: Hong Kong has launched a public consultation for its first five-year plan in a politically symbolic step that brings it closer to mainland China’s development approach.

video:video.video:title: South Africa marks 50 years since Soweto uprising, but challenges linger for its youth
video:video.video:description: South Africa on Tuesday marked the 50th anniversary of the Soweto uprising when over 200 young people protesting against the apartheid education system were shot and killed by the police.

video:video.video:title: Paris Hilton backs Provo Canyon lawsuits, says change is needed
video:video.video:description: Paris Hilton returned Monday to the Utah boarding school where she said she was abused as a teenager on the latest stop in her yearslong campaign calling for reforms to the so-called troubled teen industry.

video:video.video:title: Infamous WWII-era 'Death Railway' station resurfaces after decades underwater
video:video.video:description: Remnants of Nithe Station have resurfaced from beneath a reservoir as water levels dipped. Thousands of Allied prisoners of war and Asian laborers died while building the railway for the occupying Japanese forces in Southeast Asia.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 15
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, June 15th: United States and Iran reach initial agreement; Trump heads to G7 summit in France; Ukraine says Russian attack hit centuries-old religious site; 11 skydivers dead in Missouri plane crash.

video:video.video:title: Shipwreck reveals Roman Empire's commercial ties with Hungary
video:video.video:description: Artefacts recovered from a Roman shipwreck found off the coast of present-day Croatia are giving historians new insights into ancient trade networks.

video:video.video:title: World leaders welcome tentative deal on ending the Iran war
video:video.video:description: World welcomed an initial agreement early Monday between the United States and Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz and further extend a shaky ceasefire in the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Deadly Russian attack hits Kyiv and damages centuries-old religious site
video:video.video:description: A large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine on Monday killed rescue workers and sparked a fire Monday at one of the country’s most significant religious landmarks, a monastic complex of golden domes in the heart of Kyiv.

video:video.video:title: Starmer says Britain will ban under-16s from using a range of social media apps
video:video.video:description: Britain will ban under-16s from using a range of social media apps, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Monday, calling it “a big moment for our country.”

video:video.video:title: Tehran residents react to initial agreement with US to end war
video:video.video:description: Tehran residents on Monday cautiously welcomed an initial agreement between Iran and the United States to further extend a shaky ceasefire in the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: 8 people died in B-52 bomber crash at US Air Force base in Southern California, officials say
video:video.video:description: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff at a U.S. Air Force base in Southern California’s Mojave Desert and burst into flames Monday, killing all eight people aboard, military officials said.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy arrives in Geneva to attend the G7 summit
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Geneva on Monday evening to join the leaders of the G7 countries.

video:video.video:title: 6.7 magnitude earthquake hits part of Indonesia, followed by strong aftershocks
video:video.video:description: A 6.7 magnitude earthquake shook part of Indonesia and was followed by strong aftershocks. The initial temblor caused strong shaking lasting more than a minute.

video:video.video:title: The 700-year-old St. Vitus Cathedral in Prague inaugurates a new organ
video:video.video:description: Prague’s St. Vitus Cathedral had its new organ inaugurated on Monday, giving the 700-year-old building, the largest in the Czech Republic, a proper instrument to accompany religious services and concerts.

video:video.video:title: Pont Neuf Cavern by artist JR opens to visitors in Paris
video:video.video:description: One of the most ambitious public artworks Paris has seen in decades - the Pont Neuf Cavern by artist JR - opened to the public on Monday evening.

video:video.video:title: Russian Tu-22M3 bomber crashes during training flight in Siberia
video:video.video:description: According to the military, the aircraft crashed during a training flight and was not carrying any ammunition.

video:video.video:title: Jeremy Allen White sad to say goodbye to 'The Bear'
video:video.video:description: Jeremy Allen White says it's hard to say goodbye to hit drama "The Bear," but says he's found a "great confidence" during filming.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 17
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 16th: U.S.-Iran deal moves toward formal signing; Trump attends G7 summit in France; 8 dead in U.S. military plane crash; Protests outside Iran’s World Cup soccer game.

video:video.video:title: Solar cold storage helps African farmers cut losses and reach global markets
video:video.video:description: Kenyan smallholder farmers are reducing post-harvest losses and accessing global export markets through a pay-per-use solar cold storage model pioneered by Nairobi-based company SoKo Fresh.

video:video.video:title: German chancellor Merz gives Trump a number 47 soccer jersey at G7 summit
video:video.video:description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Tuesday gave U.S. President Donald Trump a soccer jersey with his name and the number 47 on the back.

video:video.video:title: Mixed emotions ahead of Haiti vs. Brazil for dually aligned fans headed to the World Cup game
video:video.video:description: Haitians are feeling mixed emotions as their team faces Brazil in the World Cup. Fans have long supported Brazil, drawn by cultural similarities and shared history.

video:video.video:title: As Iran chases the World Cup, its US diaspora is divided between protesting and cheering
video:video.video:description: Iranian American demonstrators amassed outside Iran’s first World Cup match, waving the pre-revolutionary lion-and-sun flags banned inside the stadium and calling for change in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: G7 leaders to discuss Ukraine on first full day of meetings at summit
video:video.video:description: Leaders of the Group of Seven are discussing Russia's war in Ukraine as they meet for the first full day at the G7 summit of leading industrialized nations on Tuesday in the French town of Evian-les-Bains.

video:video.video:title: Should you recycle? Here's how it really works
video:video.video:description: Many people are skeptical that recycling makes a difference. To find out what happens after materials leave the curb, we visited one of the largest recycling facilities in North America, where more than 1,000 tons of recyclables are processed each day.

video:video.video:title: 'The Pont Neuf Cave' artwork opens
video:video.video:description: "La Caverne du Pont Neuf" ("The Pont Neuf Cave") by artist JR, opened to the Parisian public on Monday.

video:video.video:title: German nonprofit promotes organ donation with tattoo campaign
video:video.video:description: In Germany, where thousands of patients have remained on transplant waiting lists, a non-profit organization is turning support for organ donation into something permanent: a tattoo.

video:video.video:title: Picture wall of part of centuries-old Lahore Fort completed
video:video.video:description: Conservation efforts to restore the Picture Wall on the northern section of the centuries old Lahore Fort in Pakistan has been completed after more than a decade of work.

video:video.video:title: Fisherman catches white shark, removes hook and releases the top predator fish off Massachusetts
video:video.video:description: A fisherman in Massachusetts reeled in and released a nine-foot white shark. Elliot Sudal, a veteran angler and boat captain, caught the shark – also known as a great white shark or a great white – on Nantucket.

video:video.video:title: Merz gifts Trump German soccer jersey reading 'Trump 47' at G7 summit
video:video.video:description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Tuesday gave U.S. President Donald Trump a soccer jersey with his name and the number 47 on the back.

video:video.video:title: Officials say eight people died in B-52 bomber crash at US Air Force base in Southern California
video:video.video:description: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff at a U.S. Air Force base in Southern California’s Mojave Desert and burst into flames Monday, killing all eight people aboard, military officials said.

video:video.video:title: Part of the World War ll-era 'Death Railway' resurfaces in Thailand after 40 years underwater
video:video.video:description: One of the major stations from World War II’s infamous “Death Railway” has resurfaced after decades underwater in mainland Southeast Asia and researchers are racing to western Thailand to survey the remnants of the site.

video:video.video:title: 'The Bear' cast says goodbye to the show
video:video.video:description: Jeremy Allen White, Ayo Edebiri, Ebon Moss-Bachrach and Will Poulter talk about the personal impact of working on "The Bear" as they premiere the hit show's fifth and final season in New York.

video:video.video:title: The G7 papier-maché heads are back
video:video.video:description: Oxfam activists wearing papier mache heads depicting the G7 leaders made an appearance near the 2026 G7 summit location for a satirical, but sedate, protest.

video:video.video:title: Trump ‘not happy’ with Israel’s handling of Hezbollah and Lebanon
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday said he is “not happy with the way Israel has handled themselves with Lebanon and with Hezbollah.”

video:video.video:title: Republican Gov. Mike DeWine says Ohio should abolish the death penalty, saying it is not a deterrent
video:video.video:description: Gov. Mike DeWine says he believes Ohio should abolish the death penalty. The term-limited governor, who has repeatedly postponed scheduled executions, said Tuesday that data shows the death penalty is not serving as a deterrent to violent crime.

video:video.video:title: Iranians protest ahead of Iran-New Zealand World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Iranian Americans are torn over Iran’s participation in the World Cup, as some community members plan to watch Monday’s match and others are protesting outside the stadium where the team will play.

video:video.video:title: Filmmaker Curry Barker on the wild box office success of 'Obsession'
video:video.video:description: Over the last month, “Obsession” has sent shock waves through Hollywood as it has grossed $286 million worldwide. 26-year-old filmmaker Curry Barker ponders how his micro-budget horror film has become one of the most profitable movies ever made.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran deal will 'fully open' Strait of Hormuz 'toll-free' by Friday
video:video.video:description: Speaking on the sidelines of the G7 summit in France, President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will be fully reopened and toll-free by Friday as the U.S. and Iran prepare to sign a ceasefire agreement and begin talks on Tehran’s nuclear program.

video:video.video:title: Congo is ready for its first World Cup appearance in 53 years
video:video.video:description: Congo is optimistic ahead of its World Cup opening match against Portugal. It's the team's first appearance in the tournament in 53 years.

video:video.video:title: Paris Hilton wants to be a soccer mom
video:video.video:description: Paris Hilton wants to be a soccer mom. She was in the crowd as the U.S. began its World Cup run with a 4-1 win over Paraguay.

video:video.video:title: Krys Marshall on filming 'Paradise' while pregnant
video:video.video:description: Here's what makes the second season of "Paradise" special — and deeply personal — for Krys Marshall.

video:video.video:title: Krys Marshall on filming 'Paradise' while pregnant
video:video.video:description: Here's what makes the second season of "Paradise" special — and deeply personal — for Krys Marshall.

video:video.video:title: Soccer fans spend time with Tim Howard on World Cup field
video:video.video:description: A few lucky fans stood pitchside with former American soccer player Tim Howard as the U.S. began its World Cup run with a 4-1 win over Paraguay last week.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse footage shows smoke from B-52 deadly crash at US Air Force base in Southern California
video:video.video:description: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff at a U.S. Air Force base in Southern California’s Mojave Desert and burst into flames Monday, killing all eight people aboard, military officials said.

video:video.video:title: Trump at G7 summit for talks with world leaders on Iran and Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Leaders of the Group of Seven gathered on Tuesday with partner nations to discuss Russia's war in Ukraine and U.S.

video:video.video:title: Rescued grizzly brothers enjoy snow day at San Diego Zoo
video:video.video:description: Rescued grizzly bear brothers Scout and Montana spent the day exploring, wrestling and playing in man-made snow added to their habitat to recreate conditions they might experience in the wild, according to the San Diego Zoo Wildlife Alliance.

video:video.video:title: A taste of home, yerba mate is a shared bond for many World Cup fans
video:video.video:description: Alongside their flags, jerseys and songs, fans coming the World Cup matches will bring along their yerba mate, a beverage that is ubiquitous in some South American countries and has gone global in other cultures.

video:video.video:title: Iran says the deal to end the war with the US requires Israel to withdraw from Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Iran’s Foreign Minister says the tentative agreement to end the war with the United States would require Israel to withdraw from Lebanon — a condition Israel has already rejected.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he hasn't been briefed on alleged plot against White House UFC event
video:video.video:description: Speaking on the sidelines of the G7 summit, President Donald Trump said he had not been briefed on an alleged plot targeting the UFC event at the White House on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Iraq World Cup squad celebrate birth of defender Frans Putros' newborn
video:video.video:description: The Iraq national team celebrated the arrival of defender Frans Putros' newborn son, just a day before opening their World Cup match against Norway.

video:video.video:title: Iranians protest ahead of Iran-New Zealand World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Iranian Americans are torn over Iran’s participation in the World Cup, as some community members plan to watch Monday’s match and others are protesting outside the stadium where the team will play.

video:video.video:title: Excitement builds in Dhaka as Bangladeshi fans of Argentina look forward to World Cup opener
video:video.video:description: Bangladeshi fans of Argentina rallied Tuesday in Dhaka, carrying Argentine flags and wearing the team’s jersey as World Cup fever hits the nation.

video:video.video:title: Cincinnati's Scott Satterfield on Brendan Sorsby's chances of going in NFL supplemental draft
video:video.video:description: Cincinnati coach Scott Satterfield on Brendan Sorsby's chances of going in the NFL supplemental draft.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang urges society to adapt to new norms in the age of AI
video:video.video:description: In an exclusive interview with The Associated Press, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says the AI age demands new social norms and encourages people to embrace the technology's potential instead of fearing it, insisting it will create exciting "new opportunities."

video:video.video:title: Pool owners react to algae bloom turning Trump's reflecting pool green
video:video.video:description: An algae bloom has turned the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool green days after the completion of President Donald Trump's renovation project that aimed for the shade "American flag blue."

video:video.video:title: Protesters clash with US Marshals in St. Paul, Minnesota
video:video.video:description: Dozens of protesters gathered outside a federal courthouse in St. Paul Minnesota to denounce immigration enforcement-related arrests; federal officers in tactical gear deployed pepper spray at the group.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia's Huang says society needs 'new social norms' in age of AI
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang — whose work helped enable artificial intelligence — is stressing in an Associated Press interview that society has no choice but to change in the advent of AI.

video:video.video:title: AP talks to Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang
video:video.video:description: The Associated Press gives its takeaways after an interview with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on everything from AI to President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Federal prosecutors charged 15 people with impeding agents during Minnesota immigration crackdown
video:video.video:description: The charges come as the Trump administration has escalated its attacks on “antifa,” an umbrella term for a diffuse movement of militant left-wing activists, which President Donald Trump has described as a domestic terror group.

video:video.video:title: Soccer fans spend time with Tim Howard on World Cup field
video:video.video:description: A few lucky fans stood pitchside with former American soccer player Tim Howard as the U.S. began its World Cup run with a 4-1 win over Paraguay last week.

video:video.video:title: Kennedy orders cruise ship passenger to stay in quarantine, outraging some advocates and lawyers
video:video.video:description: U.S. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has refused to release a cruise ship passenger exposed to hantavirus from a quarantine facility in Nebraska.

video:video.video:title: Few residents return to Lebanon's Nabatiyeh after a US-Iran truce with fighting nearby
video:video.video:description: Since the early days of the latest Israel-Hezbollah war, the ancient city of Nabatiyeh has been subjected to intense airstrikes and shelling that have killed and wounded scores of people.

video:video.video:title: South Africa marks 50 years since Soweto uprising by students, but challenges linger for its youth
video:video.video:description: The events of June 16, 1976 are now commemorated annually as Youth Day. Fifty years after the uprising, many from South Africa’s “born free” generation, those born after the end of apartheid in 1994, still face inequality, unemployment and poverty.

video:video.video:title: Hot mic captures Giorgia Meloni telling G7 leaders that she quit smoking one month ago
video:video.video:description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni told fellow G7 leaders on Tuesday she had quit smoking. While waiting for US President Donald Trump, French President Emmanuel Macron and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to arrive for a meeting, G7 leaders struck up a conversation about smoking.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang tries to quell fears on future of technology
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang — whose work helped enable artificial intelligence — is stressing in an Associated Press interview that society has no choice but to change in the advent of AI.

video:video.video:title: 7 patients said to have recovered from Ebola leave DR Congo treatment center
video:video.video:description: Seven people said to have recovered from Ebola were released from a treatment center in DR Congo on Tuesday, according to local health officials from the humanitarian organization Alliance for International Medical Action (ALIMA).

video:video.video:title: Ebola fears drive pregnant women away from hospitals in DR Congo
video:video.video:description: Since the outbreak of the Ebola epidemic in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, pregnant women have begun to avoid hospitals for fear of becoming infected or being placed in quarantine.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang discusses AI policy, manufacturing and Trump relationship
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stressed in an Associated Press interview Tuesday that society needs to change with the advent of AI, arguing that a fuller embrace of the technology would improve people's lives.

video:video.video:title: African and Commonwealth nations in Kenya urge quick execution of key treaty protecting oceans
video:video.video:description: African and Commonwealth nations called Tuesday for a swift implementation of a landmark treaty protecting the high seas, warning that despite record commitments to marine conservation, much of the world’s ocean protection still exists only on paper.

video:video.video:title: Romanian secret police recordings from when Ceausescu regime fell in exhibition
video:video.video:description: Former Securitate interrogation recordings from as late as December 1989 when the Ceaușescu regime fell after a bloody revolution, are now on display in an exhibition hosted by National History Museum in Bucharest.

video:video.video:title: 1 killed when small plane crashes on Texas highway. People leave vehicles to try to help
video:video.video:description: A small plane crashed in the middle of a highway in southern Texas. Crews rushed Tuesday night to pull out people from the aircraft as flames burst from the plane.

video:video.video:title: Argentina fans celebrate Messi-inspired win over Algeria
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Argentina supporters gathered at a Fan Zone in Buenos Aires to watch the defending champions defeat Algeria 3-0 in its opening match of the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: A right-wing backlash surges in Latin America as crime fears fuel Bukele-style crackdowns
video:video.video:description: At the start of this decade, Latin America leaned left as progressive politicians gained power amid public outrage over inequities worsened by the pandemic.

video:video.video:title: Tiny pony recovers in a fourth-floor apartment in Croatia
video:video.video:description: A month-old pony named Mile is the newest resident of an apartment building in the northern Croatian coastal town of Rovinj.

video:video.video:title: Host Broadcaster hot mics give glimpse of personal interactions between G7 leaders at summit
video:video.video:description: At the G7 in Evian, France, intimate moments between world leaders were carried by the French G7 Host Broadcaster. Conversations between U.S.

video:video.video:title: Norwegian, Iraqi soccer fans optimistic over their teams after World Cup soccer match
video:video.video:description: Norwegian and Iraqi soccer fans say they are optimistic over their teams after their first match in the FIFA World Cup. Norway won 4-1 over Iraq in Group I.
